label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this locker problem. So, there are 100 lockers, all starting closed. Then there are 100 people, each person k toggles every k-th locker. Toggling means if it's closed, they open it, and if it's open, they close it. The question is, how many lockers are open at the end? Hmm. Let me start by thinking about what happens to a single locker. Let's take a specific locker, say locker number 12. Which people will toggle it? Well, person 1 toggles every locker, so locker 12 is toggled by person 1. Then person 2 toggles every 2nd locker, so locker 12 is toggled again. Person 3 toggles every 3rd locker, so locker 12 is toggled again (since 12 is divisible by 3). Then person 4 toggles every 4th locker, so locker 12 is toggled again. Similarly, person 6, person 12 would toggle it. So, how many times is locker 12 toggled?
